title: Game of Life
hints: Since updates must be simultaneous, you cannot modify the input grid in place while reading from it. Consider creating a separate structure to store the result.
Iterate through every cell in the grid. For each cell, count its live neighbors by checking the eight surrounding positions, being careful with boundary checks.
A live cell survives with 2 or 3 neighbors; a dead cell is born with exactly 3. All other cases result in death. Apply these rules to build the new grid.
